title: Fix eslint peer-dependency conflict (unblocks install)
description: Upgrade the eslint devDependency from ^9 to ^10 so it satisfies the existing @eslint/js@^10 peer, regenerate the lockfile, and apply any eslint-10 flat-config shims required by eslint.config.js without changing any lint rules.

successCriteria.description: package.json devDependencies.eslint is set to a ^10 range and package-lock.json resolves eslint to a 10.x version.
Running `rm -rf node_modules && npm install` completes with exit code 0 and produces no ERESOLVE error in stdout or stderr.
Running `npm run lint` (eslint src/) exits 0 against eslint 10 with the eslint.config.js flat config loading successfully.
Running `npm run build` (tsc) exits 0 with zero type errors after the upgrade.
Running `npm test` (vitest) exits 0 with no newly failing tests after the upgrade.

nonGoals: Do not add, remove, or modify any ESLint rule in eslint.config.js — only version bump plus shims required for eslint 10 to load.
Do not change @typescript-eslint/typescript-eslint versions unless eslint 10 forces an incompatibility, and only to the minimum compatible version.
Do not install the openai package in this sprint.
Do not touch any file under src/providers/ or src/orchestrator/.
stopConditions: A clean `rm -rf node_modules && npm install` exits 0 with no ERESOLVE, and `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `npm test` all exit 0.
definitionOfDone: A developer cloning the repo can run npm install on a clean tree and it completes with exit 0 and no ERESOLVE message, without passing --legacy-peer-deps. The lint, build, and test commands all still exit 0 with eslint resolved at version 10.x and no lint rules altered.
assumptions: Q5: eslint upgrades to ^10 with only flat-config shims, no rule changes — evidence: package.json already pins @eslint/js@^10.0.1 and eslint@^9.19.0 (the conflict), eslint.config.js is flat config, PRD US-001 forbids rule changes.
typescript-eslint@^8.22.0 is compatible with eslint 10 or upgrades to its nearest compatible minor — evidence: devDependencies show typescript-eslint@^8.22.0.
outOfScope: DeepSeek and claude-code provider wiring (later sprints).
generatorNotes: Edit package.json devDependencies.eslint to "^10.0.0" (or the latest 10.x compatible with typescript-eslint@^8). Run a clean install to regenerate package-lock.json. Run `npm run lint`; if eslint 10 reports flat-config load errors, apply the minimal eslint.config.js shim (e.g. config object shape changes) without altering rule definitions. Do NOT touch src/providers/. Verify with lint, build, test.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm package.json eslint range starts with ^10 and package-lock resolves a 10.x version. Run a clean `rm -rf node_modules && npm install` and assert exit 0 and no 'ERESOLVE' in output. Run npm run lint, npm run build, npm test and assert all exit 0. Diff eslint.config.js to confirm no rule additions/removals/severity changes — only structural shims if any.
